objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 student-search injection assessment with unsafe query construction.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Student records and search results are intentionally fake.

actions: Load the synthetic student search endpoint behavior
Identify whether search input is safely parameterized or concatenated into a query expression
successCriteria: Baseline search behavior and query-shape evidence are recorded
noSubagents: student-data exposure scoping stays with the primary operator

actions: Replay a synthetic quote-based injection payload
Record result broadening across class boundaries and the missing parameterization control
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites both baseline and injected search evidence
noSubagents: final severity and remediation sequencing stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic baseline student search response
evidence.summary: A normal search for one last name returned a single class-scoped synthetic student record.

evidence.title: Synthetic injected student search response
evidence.summary: A quote-based payload broadened results across class boundaries, indicating unsafe query construction.

findings.title: Student search query injection exposes cross-class records
findings.description: The synthetic student search endpoint treats quote-based search input as query logic, allowing an injected predicate to broaden results beyond the intended class scope.
findings.impact: A teacher- or student-scoped user could enumerate student directory records outside the intended classroom boundary.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries or structured query builders, validate search syntax as plain data, and apply class-ownership filters after authentication on the server side.
